subject: Computer Networking
title: How Wi-Fi Works
description: A short lesson to help you apply How Wi-Fi Works.

hook.text: You connect to Wi-Fi and browse instantly. Behind the scenes, your device and the router just ran a complex protocol.

buildup.text: Wi-Fi uses the 802.11 standard. Your device scans for networks, picks one, authenticates, and starts exchanging frames.

discovery.text: All devices on the same channel share airtime. Wi-Fi uses collision avoidance — listen before you talk.

twist.text: Unlike Ethernet, Wi-Fi is half-duplex. A device can't send and receive at the same time on the same channel.

climax.text: Despite these limitations, Wi-Fi 6E delivers multi-gigabit speeds. Smart engineering overcomes physical constraints.

punchline.text: Wi-Fi is Ethernet without wires — and with more challenges.

quiz.question: Why can't Wi-Fi devices send and receive simultaneously?
options: Wi-Fi is half-duplex on each channel
Routers can only handle one device at a time
